title: Latest news and events
intro: News and events from across the NSW Government.

cards.title: Nominations open for Green Globe Awards 2026
cards.summary: The annual awards recognise individuals and organisations for leadership, commitment and innovation in sustainability across NSW.
cards.imageAlt: Sunlight through a green forest canopy
cards.detail:
The Green Globe Awards are the NSW Government's premier sustainability awards, celebrating organisations and individuals driving environmental innovation across the state.

This year adds two new categories recognising circular-economy initiatives and First Nations-led conservation projects. Nominations are open to businesses, community groups, government agencies and individuals.

cards.title: Quality green spaces and a million more trees for NSW
cards.summary: The NSW Government will create more quality green spaces and increase the tree canopy in Sydney by 2027.
cards.imageAlt: Aerial view of tree-lined green parkland
cards.detail:
A new statewide greening program will deliver one million additional trees and expand quality green space within ten minutes' walk of every home in Greater Sydney.

The program prioritises suburbs with the lowest existing canopy cover, targeting cooler streets, better air quality and easier access to nature.

cards.title: New Premier's Priority to lift literacy and numeracy
cards.summary: Renewed effort to lift literacy and numeracy standards across public schools so every student can be their best.
cards.imageAlt: Students reading and writing in a bright classroom
cards.detail:
A renewed Premier's Priority sets a clear target to increase the proportion of students reaching the top two NAPLAN bands for reading and numeracy.

The plan invests in evidence-based teaching, small-group tuition and earlier identification of students who need extra support.

cards.title: Digital Restart Fund backs new citizen services
cards.summary: Investment in user-centred digital services makes it easier for people to access government online.
cards.imageAlt: Person using a laptop to access an online service
cards.detail:
The Digital Restart Fund supports agile, multi-disciplinary teams to design and deliver digital services around real user needs.

The latest round funds accessible service redesigns, identity and payments improvements, and reusable design-system components shared across agencies.

cards.title: Renewable Energy Zones reach construction milestone
cards.summary: Major progress on Renewable Energy Zones brings cleaner, cheaper and more reliable power closer to communities.
cards.imageAlt: Wind turbines on rolling green hills at sunrise
cards.detail:
Renewable Energy Zones bundle new generation, storage and transmission to modernise the grid as ageing coal stations retire.

Construction has begun on key transmission links, unlocking thousands of regional jobs and new investment in host communities.

cards.title: Support grows for small business across regional NSW
cards.summary: New advisory services and grants help small businesses start, adapt and grow in regional communities.
cards.imageAlt: Owner standing in the doorway of a small regional shop
cards.detail:
A regional small-business package combines free advisory services, digital-skills training and targeted grants.

Support focuses on helping businesses adopt online tools, reach new customers and build resilience to seasonal and economic change.

cards.title: Coastal protection program safeguards beaches
cards.summary: Investment in coastal management protects beaches, dunes and estuaries from erosion and storm impacts.
cards.imageAlt: Waves breaking on a wide sandy beach with dunes
cards.detail:
The coastal management program funds councils to plan for and respond to erosion, storms and rising sea levels.

Works include dune restoration, living-shoreline solutions and improved public access that protects sensitive habitats.

cards.title: Major hospital upgrades underway across the state
cards.summary: A statewide health infrastructure program delivers new and upgraded hospitals closer to where people live.
cards.imageAlt: Modern hospital building exterior on a clear day
cards.detail:
The health infrastructure program is delivering new emergency departments, operating theatres and inpatient units in metropolitan and regional centres.

Designs emphasise accessibility, natural light and flexible spaces that can adapt to future models of care.

cards.title: Accessible transport upgrades roll out network-wide
cards.summary: Lifts, ramps and wayfinding upgrades make public transport easier to use for everyone.
cards.imageAlt: Modern train at an accessible station platform
cards.detail:
The Transport Access Program upgrades stations and wharves with lifts, ramps, accessible toilets and clearer wayfinding.

Improvements help people with disability, older travellers and parents with prams move through the network independently.

cards.title: Statewide arts festival celebrates NSW creativity
cards.summary: A month-long festival brings music, theatre and visual arts to communities across the state.
cards.imageAlt: Crowd enjoying an outdoor evening arts performance
cards.detail:
The statewide arts festival showcases local artists across regional towns and city venues, with many free and family-friendly events.

The program champions First Nations artists, emerging talent and accessible performances with captioning and Auslan interpretation.
